phase_inference_basis: Acute auditory transformation during dosing.
raw_excerpt: The music changes, it’s so weird, going from fast to very slow, slow songs become even slower. Everything changes, becoming at once very loud or very soft.
notes: Pure auditory_change anchor for oral route. Note: contrasts Mollaahmetoglu P04 auditory which was harmonic/recognizable; here speed/loudness shifts dominate.

phase_inference_basis: Multimodal acute experience with self-described dissociation framing.
raw_excerpt: It feels like [a trip]. Like my mind is detached from my body. It goes in all directions, so to say (…) a rollercoaster of colors, light, sounds, thoughts.
notes: Multi-dimensional anchor — useful for state-vector covariance modeling.

phase_inference_basis: 'Started feeling...slowly...as it was getting worse' — explicit onset progression.
raw_excerpt: I started feeling a vibration in my body, slowly and as it was getting worse it was as if I could hear it. A kind of humming or like [the buzzing sound of] your phone vibrating.
notes: FIRST onset-phase record in dataset. Vibratory body sensation crossing into auditory — characteristic of oral esketamine onset.

phase_inference_basis: 'Suddenly, all at once, I enter this experience' — explicit ascent transition into the peak.
raw_excerpt: Suddenly, all at once, I enter this experience, I am taken along (…) I no longer have my body (…) I have no idea that I still have a body, so to speak, then it really only is my mind.
notes: FIRST ascent-phase record in dataset. Body_detachment=1.0 with abrupt onset characteristic of oral peak.

phase_inference_basis: Loss of self-as-body framing during peak.
raw_excerpt: You don't know where you are. Are you just a little cell, or are you a particle, or…? Something, but not something with two hands, two legs and a head and a torso. (…) My self is just gone. These were really experiences outside myself.
notes: identity_shift + cosmic_scale (cell/particle scale framing).

phase_inference_basis: In-session relief from depressive bodily-felt sensation.
raw_excerpt: When I’m deep in my depression (…) it feels as if my heart is literally breaking it hurts so much. (…) During the ketamine, my head is empty, but that deep feeling of depression that is not so much in my head, but in my body, is also gone. So that gives a lot of relief.
source_theme_label: Detachment — From negative emotion / interruption of negativity
notes: Anchor for 'interruption of depressive somatic-affective load' as a peak-phase mechanism.

phase_inference_basis: In-session reframing of waking life as dream.
raw_excerpt: You actually end up in a sort of different reality (…) [It is] a kind of waking up. [In the sense] that our daily life is a kind of dream state, a sort of program, an automatic thing (…) ketamine makes you aware of.
notes: insight=0.75 with narrative_coherence preserved — 'lucid mystical' archetype variant.

phase_inference_basis: Story-of-self dissolution + 'this is it!' realization.
raw_excerpt: The story of [me] as a patient lying here (…) completely disappeared. (…) Just a total openness (…) a kind of primal feeling which I cannot quite describe, a clear feeling of ‘this is it!’ This unity, where for a moment [my story] diminishes. Not as a thought, more as a kind of realization. And in a pleasant way.
notes: Mystical-positive archetype variant where identity_shift is framed as story-loss not body-loss.

phase_inference_basis: Floating-toward-light imagery during peak.
raw_excerpt: When I am carried along and upwards towards the light [it is all] just peaceful, light, and it just feels just nice, and then I feel myself [floating] like a feather all the way up.
notes: Same participant as P4_peak_01 (body loss) — different session/moment, different valence. Same person, different archetype within sessions.

phase_inference_basis: 'Completely gone... not able to move... not here on earth anymore' — total loss of agency and embodiment.
raw_excerpt: I'm just really completely gone. (…) I don't perceive the outside world either. I'm not able to move. (…) I'm not here, on earth, anymore. I'm floating somewhere in that infinite world that doesn't end.
notes: FIRST k_hole-phase record in dataset. Multiple dimensions saturated. Valence neutral — neither terrifying nor blissful here.

phase_inference_basis: Self-diminishment with sense of unity at peak.
raw_excerpt: You step outside of your particular little personhood; you get a kind of broader perspective (…) The sense of unity was very strong there (…) so the person you are necessarily does lose a bit of its importance.
notes: identity_shift=true via personhood-loss framing rather than body-loss.

phase_inference_basis: Pattern-seeing / aesthetic-perfection insight at peak.
raw_excerpt: It's like the golden ratio, which recurs a lot in nature, in shells and so on, which comes back to perfection, which I actually experienced, the perfection of everything, a kind of unity.
notes: Aesthetic-perfection variant of the mystical archetype.

phase_inference_basis: Verbal-content insight emerging during peak.
raw_excerpt: Suddenly, I heard - I can't say I heard a voice, but a phrase came into my head: dissolved in the All. (…) and an enormous sense of peace came over me.
notes: entity_or_presence=true (phrase 'came into head' ambiguous between auditory hallucination and mystical knowing — coded as quasi-presence).

phase_inference_basis: Visual + auditory hallucination with preserved partial meta-awareness.
raw_excerpt: Sometimes I knew that the things I saw or heard were not really there. For example, I once thought that a blanket was a monster, or that I heard a scream outside. And although I was still able to think they were not real, it does feel real, and it did make me afraid ... and it’s hard work to stay with it.
notes: Preserved partial meta-awareness alongside high fear — important profile distinct from full ego-dissolution fear.

phase_inference_basis: 'Time just doesn't exist...can't think anymore...just in that darkness and I can't leave' — deep dissociative trap.
raw_excerpt: Sometimes I have a bit of a horror experience: I can't get out and I don't know how long it will last because time just doesn't exist. (…) At first I think: shit, did I take too much? And then it goes wrong, because it all goes so fast and at a certain point, I can't think anymore. Then I’m just in that darkness and I can't leave, and think: shit, this wasn't supposed to happen (…). It takes forever. It is such an unpleasant experience.
notes: Fear-dominant k_hole anchor: fear=1.0, time_distortion=1.0, narrative_coherence=0.25. medical_anxiety=true ('did I take too much?'). Distinct from P10_khole_01 (neutral valence).

phase_inference_basis: Active withdrawal/avoidance during peak.
raw_excerpt: [During the ketamine sessions] I tended to get inside myself even more [mimicking fetal position], like 'I'm not there, I don't want to be there and I'm not there'. Like if you occupy less space, you also exist less.
notes: Behavioral withdrawal as response to dissociation — identity_shift coded as 'shrinking self' rather than dissolving self.

phase_inference_basis: 'Totally lost in infinity' + meta-awareness loss.
raw_excerpt: It was something I had never experienced before. (…) I find it very difficult to describe, but I was completely out of this world, (…) I could no longer just think ‘oh this is the ketamine, I'm just lying on a bed, this is a side effect’. I was totally lost in infinity.
notes: Loss of meta-awareness ('could no longer just think this is the ketamine') is the threshold separating peak from k_hole.

phase_inference_basis: 'I think I'm dead, can't return to my body, time is endless' — death-anxiety k_hole.
raw_excerpt: All of a sudden, my mind is in all kinds of other dimensions (…) I can't find my body, it doesn't exist anymore, neither does my husband or other people. (…) I feel like a hamster in one of those wheels, and I run and run and run but nothing happens. I am stuck, I can't get out anymore, nothing else exists but me, alone in these dimensions. (…) I think I'm dead, because I can't return to my body, time is endless, time doesn't exist.
notes: Maximally-saturated k_hole record — multiple dimensions at 1.0. death_rebirth_theme=true. Use as the most extreme anchor for the k_hole archetype.

phase_inference_basis: 'This lasts at least until the afternoon and the next day' — explicit post-acute fatigue lasting into the day.
raw_excerpt: It's a very heavy feeling, like if you've been running or exercising or really exerted yourself physically (…) Then everything is heavy, I can’t manage anymore, I need to take a rest for a while I need to come to myself (….) This lasts at least until the afternoon and the next day, it's usually over, although occasionally you still feel like that…
notes: FIRST return-phase record in dataset. Pure somatic-fatigue profile, no dissociative residue.

phase_inference_basis: Same-day post-session somatic side effects.
raw_excerpt: A side-effect that I could have on the evening of the session day. And often the day after as well (…) Feeling like crap. Hungover and just not feeling like anything. Not wanting to eat either. Wobbly and nauseous, dizzy, headache. Things like that.
notes: medical_anxiety=true (the somatic distress itself becomes a worry). Anchor for the 'hungover return' profile.

phase_inference_basis: Next-day post-session fatigue + muscle pain.
raw_excerpt: I was very tired, probably because of the enormous tension and my resistance to it. I also had enormous muscle pain (…) just a stiff body. I was a bit creaky the day after and the whole afternoon afterwards I just wasn't worth a damn. (…) When I had thrown up, the day after would also be completely ruined. (…) A sore belly and a terribly wrinkly feeling, so the day after was actually gone.
notes: Higher loss_of_agency than P3/P14 returns — full day lost.

phase_inference_basis: Post-session sleep-related disturbance.
raw_excerpt: When I managed to let go, the trips were okay, only the dreams that followed are annoying. But yeah, I’m not there for sweaty feet, these are thoughts related to my depression. So those come to the foreground more. Especially [this trauma] that seems to bother me greatly. (…) [It makes me] want to stay awake because I know I'm about to get [nightmares].
notes: childhood_memory=true (trauma resurfacing in dreams). Important — return phase can carry post-session psychological burden, not just somatic.

phase_inference_basis: 'Lifting the blanket' literal afterglow framing with explicit 1.5 day duration.
raw_excerpt: I feel like I'm lying under a very heavy blanket, and because of the ketamine (…) a corner is lifted and I can look at the light. So, then my mood does improve a little, and that usually lasts about a day and a half, and then it subsides.
notes: Anchor for 'partial-lift afterglow' archetype: positive but bounded ~36 hours.

phase_inference_basis: Sustained baseline mood improvement post-treatment.
raw_excerpt: Ketamine ensures a kind of basic feeling level of, how to call it, a kind of neutral feeling of well-being. So not gloomy nor overly cheerful but just a good starting point, so to say. I have the idea that I now function more like a normal person than without the ketamine.
notes: Distinct from P10 'partial-lift' — this is sustained restoration to baseline normalcy. Anchor for 'baseline-restoration afterglow'.

phase_inference_basis: Restored emotional engagement post-treatment.
raw_excerpt: Actually [when I was severely depressed] I felt little (…) It was just dead, I had no feeling. (…) When I say this now, I feel very guilty about it. But if my daughter or husband had died, I don't even think I would have felt the sadness. (…) When I looked at my daughter, I didn't feel that I loved her. But when I look at her now, I think gee I love her so very much. And I feel it too.
notes: Same participant as P4_peak/khole — same person can have terrifying k_hole AND restorative afterglow. Important for trajectory variance modeling.

phase_inference_basis: Cumulative afterglow describing post-treatment functional improvement.
raw_excerpt: My head used to be constantly giving negative impulses (…) coming up with a thousand scenarios in my head why things would go wrong or whatever. Yeah, and that's just gone. And if it is there for a moment I can stop it, and then it actually stops. That is wonderful. (…) I can read a book now and I can listen to my wife better. You know, there is room again to ask someone how they are doing. (…) There was just no room for that. [Now I have] a desire to do things again that wasn't there before. (…) Now things have a purpose: to be happy. (…) I can't really remember ever feeling this way. It has always been survival and now it's not.
notes: Strongest afterglow record in dataset. cognitive_coherence + narrative_coherence both 1.0.

phase_inference_basis: Same-day sensory hypersensitivity post-acute.
raw_excerpt: On some days, I just notice sensitivity to small sounds (…). There are also days that, having recovered after two hours, then I can just function normally. [But on bad days] the world is more overwhelming; I am more sensitive to stimuli. Like in a supermarket I can more easily become a bit panicky, overwhelmed.
notes: Variable return profile — 'good' vs 'bad' days. Some return-phase residue includes mild dissociation + sensory hypersensitivity.
